steps.title: Keep verb and complement together
steps.content: The phrase "is called" needs the complement "the chromophore" to complete the idea. Standard English does not place punctuation between a verb and the complement that completes it.

steps.title: Reject interrupting marks
steps.content: A inserts a comma, C inserts a dash, and D inserts a semicolon between "called" and "the chromophore." Each mark wrongly interrupts the verb-complement connection. The no-punctuation version keeps the sentence grammatical. The answer is B.
